raw_response:
Disagree. Although nodular lymphocyte-predominant Hodgkin's lymphoma (NLPHL) typically doesn't involve EBV, recent studies have shown that about half of PTLD cases do present with EBV reactivation, blurring this distinction somewhat. Additionally, some forms of EBV-driven malignancies might still occur without classic symptoms seen in others, challenging our categorizations even further.

Answer choice: A - NK/T cell lymphoma. 

Despite my initial agreement, I've noted an important point regarding PTLD involving EBV, suggesting that the absence of EBV in NLPHL isn’t as absolute as previously stated. Thus, option A stands out better due to its strict association with EBV regardless of context differences across different conditions. Correcting for precision improves understanding. My final stance aligns best with A based on current research insights into EBV involvement patterns.
